timelines.title: Narendra Modi: The Frequent Flyer Timeline | Global Visits, Handshakes & Headlines (2014–2023)
timelines.description: Visual timeline mapping major international visits by Narendra Modi from 2014 onward. The infographic highlights countries visited, world leaders met, and memorable diplomatic moments across summits, bilateral talks, and strategic partnerships. Presented with humor and pop-culture energy, it turns years of international diplomacy into an easy-to-scan travel timeline featuring global meetings, symbolic moments, and recurring foreign policy themes.

timelines.title: World War II: The Global Conflict
timelines.description: A comprehensive look at the major events, battles, and turning points of the Second World War (1939-1945).

timelines.title: Japan Tour (Zen Horizons)
timelines.description: Enter a place where classic never goes out of style and technology conveys a sense of the future. This Japan tour takes you to sacred temples, classical gardens, natural hot springs and towering pagodas, as well as ultramodern Tokyo and historic Kyoto. You may be travelling by bullet train and scenic ferry, staying in ryokans and monasteries, and eating like a local. Whether it’s the electric buzz of Tokyo or the picturesque calm of Mount Koya, each stop guarantees an unforgettable encounter. It’s time to seek out those hidden shrines, the mystical monkey parks and picture-perfect towns that combine with the eye of a dream and the soul of a quest.

timelines.title: The Coffee Atlas: How a Red Berry Changed the World
timelines.description: Follow the bean. A goat herder named Kaldi dances with his flock in 9th century Ethiopia. A Yemeni monk brews the first cup to stay awake for prayers. Ottoman sultans ban it, then crown it. Venice's merchants smuggle it past the Inquisition. London's coffee houses become "penny universities" where news, money, and ideas flow like the dark brew. Brazil's emperors build an empire on its back. Seattle reinvents it for the digital age. This is not a history of coffee. This is the history of the world, caffeinated.

timelines.title: Timeless Europe
timelines.description: Experience the highlights of Central Europe on a 7-day tour through Austria, Hungary, Slovakia, the Czech Republic and Germany. Start in Vienna, then dance through the cultural heart rates of Budapest and Prague. Take in scenic rivers, fairy-tale towns, lively squares and centuries of history in each city. Between boat rides, historic castles and quaint villages, this EaseMyTrip group departure offers you shared experiences, guided tours and hassle-free border crossings. Great for first-time adventurers and experienced explorers!

timelines.title: Majestic Kerala Honeymoon Bliss
timelines.description: This relaxed journey through Kerala brings together misty hill stations, tranquil backwaters, and sun kissed coastal landscapes. Beginning in the tea-covered slopes of Munnar, the itinerary offers scenic drives, nature-led sightseeing, and time to unwind amidst cool mountain air. The experience then flows into the serene backwaters of Alleppey, followed by a coastal retreat at Poovar with mangrove cruises and quiet beach moments. Cultural exploration in Trivandrum adds depth to the journey, creating a well balanced holiday that blends nature, comfort, and gentle discovery. Ideal for travellers seeking variety without haste, this tour captures Kerala’s most loved experiences at an easy and enjoyable pace.

timelines.title: Bali Adventure Escape
timelines.description: Get ready for a week filled with smiles, sunshine, and excitement! Visit famous temples, take a swing over Ubud’s jungles, enjoy thrilling rides at Benoa Beach, and explore the stunning Nusa Penida Island. With all meals included and comfortable private transfers, it’s the stress-free family holiday you’ve been waiting for.

timelines.title: Upcoming Years Plan
timelines.description: plan for future years

timelines.title: Rise and Reign of the Great Mughal Emperor
timelines.description: This timeline explores the life, conquests, reforms, and legacy of Akbar, one of the greatest rulers of the Mughal Empire. It covers key events from his birth in 1542, accession to the throne at a young age, military expansions, administrative reforms, promotion of religious tolerance, establishment of Din-i Ilahi, and the cultural golden age under his rule, to his death in 1605. The timeline highlights how Akbar transformed the Mughal Empire into a powerful and stable kingdom in Indian history.

timelines.title: BTS ARIRANG WORLD TOUR
timelines.description: A global BTS world tour timeline covering concerts across Asia, North America, South America, Europe, and Australia from April 2026 to March 2027. The tour begins in Goyang, South Korea, and concludes in Manila, Philippines, with major stops including Tokyo, Los Angeles, London, Paris, São Paulo, Bangkok, Singapore, Melbourne, Sydney, and Hong Kong.

timelines.title: The Rise and Evolution of the Bharatiya Janata Party
timelines.description: A chronological timeline tracing the political journey of the Bharatiya Janata Party, from its ideological foundations and early organizational roots to its formation, alliances, national growth, and major electoral milestones. This timeline highlights the key phases that shaped the party’s emergence as one of India’s most influential political forces.

timelineEvents.title: The Dancing Goats of Kaldi
timelineEvents.description: Kaldi, a young goatherd in the highlands of Kaffa, notices something strange. His goats, after eating red berries from an unfamiliar shrub, dance on their hind legs and refuse to sleep at night. Curious and exhausted, Kaldi chews the berries himself. A rush of energy floods his limbs. He sees the world with new clarity. He fills his pockets and rushes down the mountain to a monastery. The monk there calls the berries "the devil's work" and throws them into the fire. But the aroma—roasting cherries, caramelizing sugars—fills the monastery. Other monks rake the beans from the embers, crush them, and pour hot water over them. They drink. And the first all-night prayer vigil begins.

timelineEvents.description: German forces under Hitler invade Poland, marking the official beginning of World War II.

timelineEvents.title: Apollo 11 Moon Landing

timelineEvents.title: The First Cup of Qahwa
timelineEvents.description: In the port city of Mocha and the surrounding monasteries of Yemen, Sufi monks face a recurring problem. Their dhikr—ritual chanting of God's names—stretches through the night. The body rebels. The eyes close. Sleep beckons. An Ethiopian trader named al-Shadhili introduces them to bunn, the dried berries of the Kaffa plant. The monks boil the beans, drink the dark liquid, and name it qahwa—that which prevents sleep, but also "wine of the bean" (the same word used for the forbidden grape wine). Unlike alcohol, this brew sharpens the mind rather than dulling it. The Sufis spread qahwa across the Arabian Peninsula. By 1500, coffee is no longer medicine. It is ritual. It is community.

timelineEvents.title: Constantinople's Caffeinated Revolution
timelineEvents.description: Two Syrian merchants—Shemsi and Hekim—open the first coffee houses in Constantinople, near the port district of Tahtakale. They call their establishments kiva han. The Ottoman Turks embrace coffee with a ferocity that alarms the powerful. Men gather not in mosques or homes, but in these liminal spaces—neither public nor private. They drink. They talk. They argue politics, poetry, and theology. The Grand Vizier tries to ban them, declaring coffee houses dens of sedition. But the Sultan himself becomes addicted. Within decades, Constantinople hosts over 600 coffee houses. A law is passed: any husband who fails to provide his wife with coffee may be divorced. Coffee has become culture.

timelineEvents.title: Bitter Medicine Crosses the Sea
timelineEvents.description: Venetian merchants, trading in Mocha cloth and Yemeni spices, notice the dark brew that Ottoman traders drink. They bring bags of "medical" coffee to Europe—marketed as a cure for scurvy, gout, and melancholy. The first European coffee arrives in Venice, where apothecaries sell it as a medicine (one florin per pound—a month's wages for a laborer). The Catholic Church is suspicious. Is this a Muslim drink? Is it an intoxicant? The debate reaches the Vatican. Pope Clement VIII demands a taste. His advisors prepare for disaster. The Pope sips. He smiles. "Why," he asks, "is this delicious drink only for infidels? Let us baptize it and make it truly Christian."

timelineEvents.title: Council of Salamanca
timelineEvents.description: Columbus presents his theories to a committee of learned men, who initially reject his geographical calculations as erroneous.

timelineEvents.title: Where Ideas Learned to Fly
timelineEvents.description: A Turkish immigrant named Pasqua Rosée opens England's first coffee house in Oxford. Students and scholars gather, paying a penny for admission and a cup. They call these establishments penny universities. No entrance exam required—just curiosity. Within twenty years, London hosts over 2,000 coffee houses. Each has a personality: Jonathan's is for stockjobbers (it becomes the London Stock Exchange). Lloyd's is for ship captains and insurers (it becomes Lloyd's of London). The Grecian is for scientists and philosophers. The Jerusalem is for coffee-house radicals who will hatch the American Revolution. Men (and some women, though banned from many) debate gravity, liberty, commerce, and the nature of kings. Charles II, terrified, tries to ban coffee houses in 1675. The protests are so fierce he revokes the ban in 11 days. Coffee has defeated a king.

timelineEvents.title: Café Procope and the Philosophes
timelineEvents.description: Francesco Procopio dei Coltelli, a Sicilian, opens Café Procope across from the Comédie-Française in Paris. He introduces something revolutionary: small marble tables, individual servings, and—most importantly—sugar. The Parisian elite arrives. Voltaire drinks forty cups of coffee a day, mixed with chocolate. Rousseau argues with Diderot about the nature of man. Benjamin Franklin writes letters to his London coffee-house friends. The American Declaration of Independence is drafted in a coffee house (the London Coffee House in Philadelphia). The French Revolution is plotted at Café Procope. A young artillery officer named Napoleon Bonaparte leaves his hat there as collateral for an unpaid coffee bill. (The hat remains. The world changes.)

timelineEvents.title: The Smuggled Seed
timelineEvents.description: For two centuries, the Arab world has guarded its coffee monopoly. No fertile bean leaves Yemen or Ethiopia. But in 1690, the Dutch governor of Malabar—an ambitious merchant named Adrian van Ommen—bribes a Yemeni trader. He leaves Mocha with several living coffee plants, hidden in the ballast of his ship. The plants survive the voyage to Batavia (modern Jakarta). Under Dutch cultivation, they thrive. The first Java coffee harvest arrives in Amsterdam in 1711. The Arab monopoly is shattered. Coffee becomes a colonial commodity—a blessing and a curse. The Dutch establish vast plantations across Java, Sumatra, and Ceylon, worked by enslaved and indentured laborers. Coffee's second act is darker than its first.

timelineEvents.title: Blood in the Coffee Fields
timelineEvents.description: By 1789, the French colony of Saint-Domingue produces half the world's coffee and sugar. The island's 500,000 enslaved Africans are worked to death—a new slave must be imported every three years because mortality is so high. On a sweltering August night, a Vodou ceremony in the Bois Caïman forest. A pig is sacrificed. An oath is sworn. The enslaved rise. The Haitian Revolution burns for thirteen years. Coffee plantations are torched. The French, Spanish, and British armies are defeated in turn. In 1804, Haiti declares independence—the first and only slave revolt to create a nation. Coffee production collapses. Napoleon, humiliated, sells his North American territory to Thomas Jefferson. The Louisiana Purchase funds Napoleon's wars and doubles the size of the United States. All because of coffee.

timelineEvents.title: The Empire Built on Beans
timelineEvents.description: Portugal's royal family flees Napoleon, decamping to Rio de Janeiro in 1808. Dom Pedro, the prince regent, loves coffee. He orders the military to distribute coffee seeds to Brazilian farmers. The soil is perfect. The climate is ideal. The labor—both enslaved and, after 1888, immigrant—is brutal and vast. By 1850, Brazil produces half the world's coffee. By 1900, it produces three-quarters. Coffee barons build railroads, cities, and political dynasties. They also create fazendas—vast plantations where workers live in debt bondage, paid in vougers for company stores. São Paulo grows from a sleepy town to South America's largest city on coffee revenues. The Brazilian Empire falls in 1889, but coffee's reign continues. To this day, Brazil produces 40% of the world's coffee.

timelineEvents.title: Direct Trade and the Search for Single Origin
timelineEvents.description: For a century, coffee is commodity—a bulk product traded on futures markets, roasted dark to mask defects, poured from diner carafes without ceremony. In the 1970s, a few roasters begin asking questions. Where does this bean come from? Who grew it? What if we roasted it lightly, to taste the soil, not hide it? A Dutch coffee importer named Frans van der Hoff travels to Chiapas and Oaxaca, bypassing the commodity system entirely. He buys directly from cooperatives, pays fair prices, and labels the bags with origin stories. "Single origin" coffee is born. In Costa Rica, farmers realize that quality matters more than quantity. They focus on honey processing, terroir, and traceability. In the United States, a Seattle-based roaster named Starbucks expands from a single Pike Place shop to a national chain—and then global phenomenon. Coffee is no longer caffeine delivery. It is ritual, craft, and story.

timelineEvents.title: The Warming Warning
timelineEvents.description: Coffee grows in a narrow band around the equator—the Bean Belt. It needs cool nights, steady rainfall, and altitudes between 3,000 and 6,000 feet. Climate change is shrinking that band. By 2050, scientists predict, half of current coffee-growing land will become unsuitable for production. Arabica—the species that produces 70% of specialty coffee—is especially vulnerable. In response, farmers are moving uphill, trying new hybrids, and reviving neglected species like Coffea stenophylla (rediscovered wild in West Africa). Shade-grown coffee becomes a climate solution, preserving forests while producing beans. Direct trade and fair certification ensure farmers receive living wages. The story of coffee is not over. The fourth wave will determine whether the bean survives us—or whether our grandchildren will drink something else entirely.

timelineEvents.title: Little Garden Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew meets giants and battles Mr. 3. Episodes 70-77.

timelineEvents.title: Visit to Russia and Austria

timelineEvents.title: Visit to Poland and Ukraine
timelineEvents.description: Historic visits to Eastern Europe

timelineEvents.title: Welcome to Vienna
timelineEvents.description: Arrival in Vienna and transfer to the hotel. Free time upon arrival. Tour information will be available on the boards at the hotel reception in the afternoon.

timelineEvents.title: City Tour of Vienna
timelineEvents.description: Panoramic city tour with a local guide covering major avenues, palaces, parks, and the city centre. Afternoon at leisure. Evening transfer to the Grinzing district for traditional taverns and dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Vienna to Budapest
timelineEvents.description: Depart for Budapest. Stop at the Váci utca area for free time and lunch. Includes a boat trip on the Danube River and a panoramic tour of Budapest. Optional evening Magyar show with dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Budapest to Prague via Bratislava
timelineEvents.description: Depart for Bratislava for free time in the historic centre. Continue to Prague. Evening transfer to Old Town Square for free time and dinner.

timelineEvents.title: City Tour of Prague
timelineEvents.description: Panoramic tour of Prague with a local guide. Visit to Prague Castle (admission included) and a boat ride on the Vltava River. Afternoon at leisure.

timelineEvents.title: Prague to Frankfurt via Pilsen and Rothenburg
timelineEvents.description: Stop in Pilsen to explore the Old Town Square and cathedral. Continue to Rothenburg along the Romantic Road. Arrive in Frankfurt for dinner in the historic city centre.

timelineEvents.title: Departure from Frankfurt
timelineEvents.description: The tour ends after breakfast.

timelineEvents.title: Drum Island Arc
timelineEvents.description: Tony Tony Chopper joins as the crew's doctor. Episodes 78-91.

timelineEvents.title: Arrival in Bali & Transfer to Ubud
timelineEvents.description: Meet & Greet at Denpasar Airport followed by a private transfer to Ubud. Check in to the hotel and enjoy the evening at leisure. Meals: Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Kintamani Volcano & Ubud Village Tour
timelineEvents.description: Full-day tour featuring Mount Batur views, Ubud Village, Monkey Forest, Bali Swing, and Tegenungan Waterfall. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Bedugul & Tanah Lot Temple Tour
timelineEvents.description: Visit Ulun Danu Beratan Temple in Bedugul and the iconic Tanah Lot Temple for sunset. Transfer and check in to Seminyak/Ubud accommodation. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Transfer to Kuta
timelineEvents.description: Check out from the hotel and transfer to Kuta for the next part of the journey. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Water Sports & Uluwatu Sunset
timelineEvents.description: Water sports at Tanjung Benoa (Parasailing, Jet Ski, Banana Boat) followed by a visit to Uluwatu Temple and a Kecak Fire Dance performance. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Nusa Penida Island Adventure
timelineEvents.description: Full-day tour of Nusa Penida including Angel’s Billabong, Broken Beach, Kelingking Beach, and Crystal Bay. Return to the main island in the evening. Meals: Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Departure from Bali
timelineEvents.description: Last-minute shopping in Kuta markets followed by a private transfer to Denpasar Airport for your onward flight. Meals: Breakfast.

timelineEvents.title: Trilateral tour (Cyprus, Canada, Croatia)
timelineEvents.description: Scheduled diplomatic visits

timelineEvents.title: Multi-nation tour 2025
timelineEvents.description: Ghana, Trinidad & Tobago, Argentina, Brazil, Namibia

timelineEvents.title: Visit to UK and Maldives
timelineEvents.description: Bilateral relations

timelineEvents.title: East Asia tour (Japan, China)
timelineEvents.description: Scheduled visits

timelineEvents.title: Middle East and Africa tour
timelineEvents.description: Jordan, Ethiopia, Oman

timelineEvents.title: Official visit to Malaysia
timelineEvents.description: Diplomatic engagement

timelineEvents.title: Strategic partnership in Israel
timelineEvents.description: Bilateral relations

timelineEvents.title: European and UAE trade tour
timelineEvents.description: UAE, Netherlands, Sweden, Norway, Italy; Focus on Trade, Energy, AI, EU ties

timelineEvents.title: Marriage to Mariam-uz-Zamani
timelineEvents.description: Married the daughter of Raja Bharmal of Amber, establishing a powerful alliance between the Mughals and the Rajputs.

timelineEvents.title: Abolition of Jizya
timelineEvents.description: Abolished the tax on non-Muslim subjects, signaling a policy of religious tolerance and secularism.

timelineEvents.title: Siege of Chittorgarh
timelineEvents.description: Led a successful military campaign against the Mewar Kingdom to expand the empire into Rajasthan.

timelineEvents.title: Foundation of Fatehpur Sikri
timelineEvents.description: Began construction of a new capital city to honor the Sufi saint Salim Chishti.

timelineEvents.title: Conquest of Gujarat
timelineEvents.description: Annexed the wealthy coastal province of Gujarat, gaining control over vital trade routes to the West.

timelineEvents.title: Battle of Haldighati
timelineEvents.description: Akbar's forces, led by Raja Man Singh, fought the forces of Maharana Pratap of Mewar.

timelineEvents.title: Proclamation of Din-i-Ilahi
timelineEvents.description: Introduced a new syncretic faith combining elements of various religions to unify his subjects.

timelineEvents.title: Annexation of Kashmir
timelineEvents.description: Incorporated the Kashmir region into the Mughal Empire.

timelineEvents.title: Death of Akbar
timelineEvents.description: Died following a period of illness; he was succeeded by his son, Jahangir, and buried at Sikandra.

timelineEvents.title: Birth of Ayush Dubey
timelineEvents.description: Ayush Dubey was born on December 2nd, 2001.

timelineEvents.title: Commenced MCA Studies
timelineEvents.description: Enrolled in the Master of Computer Applications (MCA) program in Nagpur.

timelineEvents.title: Current Residence
timelineEvents.description: Ayush Dubey currently resides in Nagpur.

timelineEvents.title: Alabasta Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew helps Vivi save her kingdom from Crocodile. Episodes 92-130.

timelineEvents.title: Jaya Arc
timelineEvents.description: Luffy meets Blackbeard and learns about the Sky Island. Episodes 144-152.

timelineEvents.title: Skypiea Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew travels to the island in the sky and fights Enel. Episodes 153-195.

timelineEvents.title: Long Ring Long Land Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew competes in a Davy Back Fight against Foxy. Episodes 207-219.

timelineEvents.title: Water 7 Arc
timelineEvents.description: Conflict arises within the crew regarding the Going Merry. Episodes 229-263.

timelineEvents.title: Enies Lobby Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew declares war on the World Government to save Robin. Episodes 264-312.

timelineEvents.title: Post-Enies Lobby Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew gets a new ship, the Thousand Sunny, and Franky joins. Episodes 313-325.

timelineEvents.title: Thriller Bark Arc
timelineEvents.description: Brook joins the crew after they defeat Gecko Moria. Episodes 337-381.

timelineEvents.title: Sabaody Archipelago Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew is separated by Kuma of the Warlords. Episodes 385-405.

timelineEvents.title: Amazon Lily Arc
timelineEvents.description: Luffy is sent to the island of women and meets Boa Hancock. Episodes 408-421.

timelineEvents.title: Impel Down Arc
timelineEvents.description: Luffy infiltrates the great prison to save Ace. Episodes 422-452.

timelineEvents.title: Marineford Arc
timelineEvents.description: The Paramount War takes place at Marine headquarters. Episodes 457-489.

timelineEvents.title: Post-War Arc
timelineEvents.description: Luffy's flashback and the decision to train for two years. Episodes 490-516.

timelineEvents.title: Return to Sabaody Arc
timelineEvents.description: The Straw Hats reunite after two years of training. Episodes 517-522.

timelineEvents.title: Fish-Man Island Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew travels under the sea and defeats Hordy Jones. Episodes 523-574.

timelineEvents.title: Punk Hazard Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew enters the New World and forms an alliance with Law. Episodes 579-625.

timelineEvents.title: Dressrosa Arc
timelineEvents.description: The alliance fights Donquixote Doflamingo. Episodes 629-746.

timelineEvents.title: Zou Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew finds the Ninja-Pirate-Mink-Samurai Alliance. Episodes 751-779.

timelineEvents.title: Whole Cake Island Arc
timelineEvents.description: Luffy rescues Sanji from Big Mom's territory. Episodes 783-877.

timelineEvents.title: Levely Arc
timelineEvents.description: The world leaders gather while secrets of the world are revealed. Episodes 878-889.

timelineEvents.title: Wano Country Arc
timelineEvents.description: The alliance battles Kaido and Big Mom in Wano. Episodes 890-1085.

timelineEvents.title: Egghead Arc
timelineEvents.description: The crew meets Dr. Vegapunk and faces the World Government. Episodes 1086-Present.

timelineEvents.title: Arrive in Tokyo
timelineEvents.description: Transfer by shared shuttle to the hotel. Free time to explore or unwind, followed by an evening tour briefing at the hotel.

timelineEvents.title: Tokyo Sightseeing & Shinjuku Night Tour
timelineEvents.description: Guided tour visiting Zojoji Temple, Shibuya Crossing, Meiji Shrine, Imperial Palace gardens, Akihabara, and Sensoji Temple in Asakusa. Evening tour of Shinjuku's nightlife and dinner.

timelineEvents.title: Tokyo – Hiroshima – Miyajima Island
timelineEvents.description: Travel to Hiroshima by bullet train. Visit Peace Memorial Park, Atomic Bomb Dome, and Peace Memorial Museum. Take a ferry to Miyajima Island to see Itsukushima Shrine.

timelineEvents.title: Hiroshima – Matsuyama
timelineEvents.description: Ferry to Shikoku Island. Visit Matsuyama Castle via cable car and explore Ishiteji Temple. Unwind at the historic Dogo Onsen bathhouse.

timelineEvents.title: Matsuyama – Kotohira – Takamatsu – Naruto – Kobe
timelineEvents.description: Climb Kotohira Shrine, visit Ritsurin Garden in Takamatsu, and see the Naruto Bridge whirlpools. Continue to Kobe for the night.

timelineEvents.title: Kobe – Osaka – Mount Koya
timelineEvents.description: Visit the Earthquake Memorial Museum in Kobe, stop in Osaka, and proceed to Mount Koya. Visit Okuno Mausoleum and Kongobuji Temple. Stay overnight at a Buddhist monastery with meditation.

timelineEvents.title: BTS World Tour — Lima
timelineEvents.description: BTS performs two shows in Lima, Peru.

timelineEvents.title: Mount Koya – Kyoto
timelineEvents.description: Travel to Kyoto to visit Fushimi Inari Shrine, the Imperial Palace, and Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion). Evening stroll through the Gion Geisha district.

timelineEvents.title: Kyoto – Tsumago – Matsumoto – Nagano
timelineEvents.description: Explore the Edo-period village of Tsumago. Visit Matsumoto's 16th-century Crow Castle before heading to the mountain town of Nagano.

timelineEvents.title: Nagano – Jigokudani – Kusatsu – Ikaho
timelineEvents.description: Zenko-ji Temple blessing ceremony, visit Jigokudani Monkey Park to see macaques, and explore Kusatsu spa town. Overnight at a ryokan in Ikaho.

timelineEvents.title: Ikaho – Nikko – Tokyo
timelineEvents.description: Travel to Nikko to visit Toshogu Shrine, Taiyuinbyo Mausoleum, and Kanmangafuchi Abyss. Return to Tokyo in the evening.

timelineEvents.title: Departure from Tokyo
timelineEvents.description: Breakfast at the hotel followed by the conclusion of the tour and individual departures.

timelineEvents.title: Day 1 Cochin - Munnar
timelineEvents.description: Arrive at Cochin Airport or Railway Station and transfer to Munnar. Travel through Valparai with stops at Cheeyappara Waterfalls and tea plantation stretches. Check in at the hotel on arrival. Later, visit Rajamala in Eravikulam National Park (subject to forest authority regulations). Explore the Tea Museum set amid tea gardens. Take a short visit through Munnar town in the evening. Overnight stay in Munnar.

timelineEvents.title: Day 2 Explore Munnar
timelineEvents.description: After breakfast, proceed for Munnar sightseeing. Visit Mattupettu Dam, Kundala Dam and Eco point. Later, visit the Tea Museum. Overnight stay in Munnar.

timelineEvents.title: Day 3 Drive from Munnar to Alappuzha
timelineEvents.description: Check out after breakfast and drive to Alappuzha. Check in at the hotel on arrival. Spend time enjoying the backwater surroundings. Opt for a Shikara boat cruise (at own cost). Overnight stay in Alappuzha.

timelineEvents.title: Day 4 Journey from Alappuzha to Kovalam
timelineEvents.description:
Proceed to Kovalam after breakfast.
Check in at the hotel.
Explore the beaches of Kovalam and activities like kayaking, swimming, surfing (additional cost).
Spend the rest of the day at leisure by the sea.
Overnight stay in Kovalam.

timelineEvents.title: Day 5 Trivandrum Sightseeing
timelineEvents.description:
Depart after breakfast for Trivandrum sightseeing - visit Padmanabhaswamy Temple along with the Museum, Zoo, and Art Gallery.
Return to Kovalam in the evening.
Overnight stay in Kovalam.

timelineEvents.title: Day 6 Trivandrum Departure
timelineEvents.description:
Check out after breakfast.
Transfer to Trivandrum Airport or Railway Station for departure.

timelineEvents.title: Foundation of Bharatiya Jana Sangh
timelineEvents.description: Syama Prasad Mukherjee founded the Bharatiya Jana Sangh (BJS), the ideological precursor to the BJP, with the support of the RSS.

timelineEvents.title: Merger into Janata Party
timelineEvents.description: The BJS merged with several other opposition parties to form the Janata Party to oppose the Emergency imposed by Indira Gandhi.

timelineEvents.title: Official Formation of BJP
timelineEvents.description: Following internal disputes within the Janata Party regarding 'dual membership' with the RSS, the Bharatiya Janata Party was officially formed with Atal Bihari Vajpayee as its first president.

timelineEvents.title: First General Election Contested
timelineEvents.description: In its first major national election after formation, the BJP won only two seats in the Lok Sabha.

timelineEvents.title: Launch of Ram Rath Yatra
timelineEvents.description: L.K. Advani launched a nationwide procession from Somnath to Ayodhya, significantly mobilizing support and defining the party's Hindutva ideological stance.

timelineEvents.title: First BJP Government (13 Days)
timelineEvents.description: The BJP emerged as the single largest party in the general elections. Atal Bihari Vajpayee was sworn in as Prime Minister but resigned after 13 days when unable to prove a majority.

timelineEvents.title: NDA Government under Vajpayee
timelineEvents.description: The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) was formed, and Vajpayee returned as Prime Minister, leading the country through the Pokhran-II nuclear tests and the Kargil War.

timelineEvents.title: Historic Majority under Narendra Modi
timelineEvents.description: The BJP won a decisive victory in the general elections, securing 282 seats and becoming the first party in 30 years to win a majority on its own.

timelineEvents.title: Re-election with Increased Majority
timelineEvents.description: The BJP-led NDA won a second consecutive term with the BJP alone winning 303 seats, further consolidating its position as the dominant political force in India.
